name: Mark Warner for VA
subject: Unprecedented and despicable
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
We can't let this continue
|
---

| | **Trump uses Rose Garden event for extended campaign-like attack on Biden
_-- Washington Post, 7/14/20_** | [Pitch in now](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85dbd2/53d9897e/547c1bca/1c8850db/1293515814/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjY1NSJ9/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-stay?refcode=20200715_f_1_nd__655__&refcode2=ask1&amount=5")
---
Derek --

Unprecedented, despicable and embarrassing.

**Yesterday, Donald Trump used the Rose Garden to hurl false attacks at Vice President Joe Biden at what was a campaign rally thinly disguised as a "news conference."** Trump took to a podium outside the Oval Office and ranted, rambled and hurled pre-scripted attacks on Joe Biden for nearly an hour.

**Independent fact-checkers haven 't gotten all the way through the transcript yet, and they've already found 19 false or misleading statements in Trump's Rose Garden rant.**

Derek, we CANNOT allow these lies to go unchecked. We must all do our part to elect leaders up and down the ticket who will restore integrity and honesty to Washington. **Mark Warner has been fighting to hold the president accountable from day one.**
**[Chip in $19 for each of Trump's false claims that have been uncovered so far.](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85dbd2/53d9897e/547c1bca/1c8850da/1293515814/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjY1NSJ9/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-stay?refcode=20200715_f_1_nd__655__&refcode2=ask2&amount=19")**
What's worse, Donald Trump made a mockery of the dignified setting normally used for official presidential announcements. We know he is upset because he's had to cancel campaign rallies, **but it violates White House protocol to launch campaign attacks against political rivals in speeches in the Rose Garden.**

This November, let's finally put an end to Trump's lies and elect Democratic leaders like Mark Warner, who will restore integrity to Washington. **[If you have the means, will you support Mark's re-election campaign?](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85dbd2/53d9897e/547c1bca/1c8850d9/1293515814/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjY1NSJ9/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-stay?refcode=20200715_f_1_nd__655__&refcode2=ask3&amount=5")**

Thank you,

Team Warner

[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85dbd2/53d9897e/547c1bca/1c8850d8/1293515814/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjY1NSJ9/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-stay?refcode=20200715_f_1_nd__655__&refcode2=cont&amount=5")
---

|
---

1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213
Williamsburg, VA 23185

[Unsubscribe](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85dbd2/53d9897e/547c1bca/1c8850c7/1293515814/VEsD/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://act.markwarnerva.com/unsubscribe")

name: Tom Cotton
subject: The woke mob runs the New York Times
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cotton for Senate 2226 Cottondale Lane
body:
After publishing my op-ed calling for a return to law and order in our cities, the New York Times completely melted down. The newsroom was completely taken over by the radical mob, which set out to turn the paper into nothing more than a far-Left propaganda machine. Bari Weiss, an opinion columnist and editor announced her resignation in a scathing letter to the publisher. In her letter she outlines the harassment and bullying she faced from colleagues because she dared to offer a “centrist” or less radical view on issues.Click here to unsubscribe http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/servlet/OneClickOptOutServlet?m=42963691&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTgwMTE2MjIzMgS2&mt=1&rt=0
http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI5NjM2OTES1&kn=8&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=2&j=MTgwMTE2MjIzMgS2&mt=1&rt=0 Peter,

After publishing my op-ed calling for a return to law and order in our cities, the New York Times melted down.

The newsroom was taken over by the radical mob, which set out to turn the paper into nothing more than a far-Left propaganda machine.

Bari Weiss, an opinion columnist and editor announced her resignation in a scathing letter to the publisher. In her letter she outlines the harassment and bullying she faced from colleagues because she dared to offer a centrist view on issues.

Check out the letter in its entirety below to read the real story about the liberal mob that now runs the New York Times. Dear A.G.,

It is with sadness that I write to tell you that I am resigning from The New York Times.

I joined the paper with gratitude and optimism three years ago. I was hired with the goal of bringing in voices that would not otherwise appear in your pages: first-time writers, centrists, conservatives and others who would not naturally think of The Times as their home. The reason for this effort was clear: The paper’s failure to anticipate the outcome of the 2016 election meant that it didn’t have a firm grasp of the country it covers. Dean Baquet and others have admitted as much on various occasions. The priority in Opinion was to help redress that critical shortcoming.

I was honored to be part of that effort, led by James Bennet. I am proud of my work as a writer and as an editor. Among those I helped bring to our pages: the Venezuelan dissident Wuilly Arteaga; the Iranian chess champion Dorsa Derakhshani; and the Hong Kong Christian democrat Derek Lam. Also: Ayaan Hirsi Ali, Masih Alinejad, Zaina Arafat, Elna Baker, Rachael Denhollander, Matti Friedman, Nick Gillespie, Heather Heying, Randall Kennedy, Julius Krein, Monica Lewinsky, Glenn Loury, Jesse Singal, Ali Soufan, Chloe Valdary, Thomas Chatterton Williams, Wesley Yang, and many others.

But the lessons that ought to have followed the election—lessons about the importance of understanding other Americans, the necessity of resisting tribalism, and the centrality of the free exchange of ideas to a democratic society—have not been learned. Instead, a new consensus has emerged in the press, but perhaps especially at this paper: that truth isn’t a process of collective discovery, but an orthodoxy already known to an enlightened few whose job is to inform everyone else.

Twitter is not on the masthead of The New York Times. But Twitter has become its ultimate editor. As the ethics and mores of that platform have become those of the paper, the paper itself has increasingly become a kind of performance space. Stories are chosen and told in a way to satisfy the narrowest of audiences, rather than to allow a curious public to read about the world and then draw their own conclusions. I was always taught that journalists were charged with writing the first rough draft of history. Now, history itself is one more ephemeral thing molded to fit the needs of a predetermined narrative.

My own forays into Wrongthink have made me the subject of constant bullying by colleagues who disagree with my views. They have called me a Nazi and a racist; I have learned to brush off comments about how I’m “writing about the Jews again.” Several colleagues perceived to be friendly with me were badgered by coworkers. My work and my character are openly demeaned on company-wide Slack channels where masthead editors regularly weigh in. There, some coworkers insist I need to be rooted out if this company is to be a truly “inclusive” one, while others post ax emojis next to my name. Still other New York Times employees publicly smear me as a liar and a bigot on Twitter with no fear that harassing me will be met with appropriate action. They never are.

There are terms for all of this: unlawful discrimination, hostile work environment, and constructive discharge. I’m no legal expert. But I know that this is wrong.

I do not understand how you have allowed this kind of behavior to go on inside your company in full view of the paper’s entire staff and the public. And I certainly can’t square how you and other Times leaders have stood by while simultaneously praising me in private for my courage. Showing up for work as a centrist at an American newspaper should not require bravery.

Part of me wishes I could say that my experience was unique. But the truth is that intellectual curiosity—let alone risk-taking—is now a liability at The Times. Why edit something challenging to our readers, or write something bold only to go through the numbing process of making it ideologically kosher, when we can assure ourselves of job security (and clicks) by publishing our 4000th op-ed arguing that Donald Trump is a unique danger to the country and the world? And so self-censorship has become the norm.

What rules that remain at The Times are applied with extreme selectivity. If a person’s ideology is in keeping with the new orthodoxy, they and their work remain unscrutinized. Everyone else lives in fear of the digital thunderdome. Online venom is excused so long as it is directed at the proper targets.

Op-eds that would have easily been published just two years ago would now get an editor or a writer in serious trouble, if not fired. If a piece is perceived as likely to inspire backlash internally or on social media, the editor or writer avoids pitching it. If she feels strongly enough to suggest it, she is quickly steered to safer ground. And if, every now and then, she succeeds in getting a piece published that does not explicitly promote progressive causes, it happens only after every line is carefully massaged, negotiated and caveated.

It took the paper two days and two jobs to say that the Tom Cotton op-ed “fell short of our standards.” We attached an editor’s note on a travel story about Jaffa shortly after it was published because it “failed to touch on important aspects of Jaffa’s makeup and its history.” But there is still none appended to Cheryl Strayed’s fawning interview with the writer Alice Walker, a proud anti-Semite who believes in lizard Illuminati.

The paper of record is, more and more, the record of those living in a distant galaxy, one whose concerns are profoundly removed from the lives of most people. This is a galaxy in which, to choose just a few recent examples, the Soviet space program is lauded for its “diversity”; the doxxing of teenagers in the name of justice is condoned; and the worst caste systems in human history includes the United States alongside Nazi Germany.

Even now, I am confident that most people at The Times do not hold these views. Yet they are cowed by those who do. Why? Perhaps because they believe the ultimate goal is righteous. Perhaps because they believe that they will be granted protection if they nod along as the coin of our realm—language—is degraded in service to an ever-shifting laundry list of right causes. Perhaps because there are millions of unemployed people in this country and they feel lucky to have a job in a contracting industry.

Or perhaps it is because they know that, nowadays, standing up for principle at the paper does not win plaudits. It puts a target on your back. Too wise to post on Slack, they write to me privately about the “new McCarthyism” that has taken root at the paper of record.

All this bodes ill, especially for independent-minded young writers and editors paying close attention to what they’ll have to do to advance in their careers. Rule One: Speak your mind at your own peril. Rule Two: Never risk commissioning a story that goes against the narrative. Rule Three: Never believe an editor or publisher who urges you to go against the grain. Eventually, the publisher will cave to the mob, the editor will get fired or reassigned, and you’ll be hung out to dry.

For these young writers and editors, there is one consolation. As places like The Times and other once-great journalistic institutions betray their standards and lose sight of their principles, Americans still hunger for news that is accurate, opinions that are vital, and debate that is sincere. I hear from these people every day. “An independent press is not a liberal ideal or a progressive ideal or a democratic ideal. It’s an American ideal,” you said a few years ago. I couldn’t agree more. America is a great country that deserves a great newspaper.

None of this means that some of the most talented journalists in the world don’t still labor for this newspaper. They do, which is what makes the illiberal environment especially heartbreaking. I will be, as ever, a dedicated reader of their work. But I can no longer do the work that you brought me here to do—the work that Adolph Ochs described in that famous 1896 statement: “to make of the columns of The New York Times a forum for the consideration of all questions of public importance, and to that end to invite intelligent discussion from all shades of opinion.”

Ochs’s idea is one of the best I’ve encountered. And I’ve always comforted myself with the notion that the best ideas win out. But ideas cannot win on their own. They need a voice. They need a hearing. Above all, they must be backed by people willing to live by them.
Sincerely,
Bari Paid for by Cotton for Senate 2226 Cottondale Lane
Suite 200
Little Rock, AR 72202

Senator Cotton was honorably discharged from the U.S. Army. Images do not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense or any service branch.

If you would like to unsubscribe, [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931bfd4/1cd0ac28/8df20307/357c5582/492880103/VEsHBQ/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/content/Unsubscribe").

Peter, thank you so much for supporting Joe Biden's Presidential campaign.

[ ](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931bfd4/1cd0ac28/8df20307/357c5581/492880103/VEsHBA/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_footer&refcode=email-bsd-20190515host-b20_FDR_states_NA-F1_S1_C1")

This isn't the time to be complacent. If you are ready to fight for the soul of this nation, you can start by donating to elect Joe Biden by clicking the button below.

| [DONATE TO ELECT JOE BIDEN »](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931bfd4/1cd0ac28/8df20307/357c5580/492880103/VEsHBw/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_footer?refcode=JB_EM_FR_2019.08.07_B8_footer_X__F1_S1_C1__footer&amount=25")
---

We know we send a lot of emails, and we are sorry about that. The reason? We are relying on grassroots supporters like you (we're serious!).

But Peter, we don't want to bother you. If you'd like to only receive our most important emails, [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931bfd4/1cd0ac28/8df20307/357c5583/492880103/VEsHBg/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/less-emails"). To make a contribution by mail, [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931bfd4/1cd0ac28/8df20307/357c527e/492880103/VEsHAw/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://joebiden.com/contribute-by-mail/") for instructions.

We sincerely thank you for your help and support.
\- The entire Joe Biden for President team

body:
It's more important than ever to have access to the best information.

| Friend, We wanted to be sure you saw Mike's note about the Expert Insights newsletter -- an essential update from the Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health on COVID-19 and other pressing public health threats. Multiple states are reporting record case levels and implementing renewed lockdowns, and it's more important than ever to have access to the best information. That's what Expert Insights provides, and you can sign up at the link below: **[https://www.jhsph.edu/subscribe/covid-19-expert-insights-newsletter_em](http://i.mikebloomberg.com/u/click?_t=8613ca2a3316453b8f979e9fa5797d01&_m=9ff488ad76364ab8a62bc03486334eb0&_e=-D9OJl4tqk8g2j8DQUwQQj-b2VwKQnkS3BzNo9LQkZv5BwelBSQlk0PZ93oDnwHfvO77DNkOoEwtTZVMCulRk6ooVEStCL3-zzknSG-ugGTaxbJOvIJbYOuAR0KJnEKojjuOMD8Ffi6pTcXzwdqqFca9nYUEZNOpyhGzMkrGqiDrJed5x8XCbc4uTcHHnEjewEMTeOQ1SZ75S8stg-9ec355OPUt6FQvqwtub_4AKtjtQQUq9116SUrq06-I2LaKLt0xpUThFnkND014fQpBTiYfHh-xWwPfeGf-of713crIdBzLioIBLA4Z2R35qAdpzc1sgEJnLvmitY8y4a90ckHXHI4A6YuNATtn98pPxlUnTz2lkCWvpdZ2l96LfX5LKihrjVQN7yXhxjNqfnpz4w%3D%3D)** As Mike says, "COVID-19 is not going away." Take a look at his personal message below and sign up for regular updates from some of the best public health experts in the world. Thanks,

Bloomberg Philanthropies
_\---Begin forwarded message---_
[](http://i.mikebloomberg.com/u/click?_t=8613ca2a3316453b8f979e9fa5797d01&_m=9ff488ad76364ab8a62bc03486334eb0&_e=-D9OJl4tqk8g2j8DQUwQQpmckw3vZMNQ7ei4fvekG_4InNMp5fi7PqJwgnLaeQXuofKwxuPwGwpwdMoACShxWBKVBhttz8U2B-VLyQCVge0fJ56ZxGNKSbFduZWgj3PEqV4v_9iTPXR-AvTmbNAF_BtMPwRHIfjT_qYqPL1iJH_s5_Afj9Ifu9FgflsFFsH4OZDvTbk7sPhkXq3Rnx279ljUwiMQMGhvqxBWhK7CYRqany3r-mQWA34qLdPD6P16yAx9cgel895hkEAOj3X_KmqNgrGsr5V_9zXLYO9ld3hkf1Mn8yKvEddme6gmaNZo) Friend, COVID-19 is not going away. And for all that we've learned about the virus, we still face a lot of uncertainty -- as cases continue to rise in many parts of the country, as families discuss summer travel, and as school communities discuss how best and when to reopen.

The scientists at the Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health have been the world's go-to experts throughout the pandemic, and they share what they learn about COVID-19 and other urgent public health issues in a regular email called Expert Insights.

It's available to everyone, and I encourage you to sign up for it: **[https://www.jhsph.edu/subscribe/covid-19-expert-insights-newsletter_em](http://i.mikebloomberg.com/u/click?_t=8613ca2a3316453b8f979e9fa5797d01&_m=9ff488ad76364ab8a62bc03486334eb0&_e=-D9OJl4tqk8g2j8DQUwQQj-b2VwKQnkS3BzNo9LQkZv5BwelBSQlk0PZ93oDnwHfvO77DNkOoEwtTZVMCulRk6ooVEStCL3-zzknSG-ugGTaxbJOvIJbYOuAR0KJnEKojjuOMD8Ffi6pTcXzwdqqFca9nYUEZNOpyhGzMkrGqiDrJed5x8XCbc4uTcHHnEjelJQ4JM2F4ap8-761B_cSELjNTW4UATPM0cSx9d-Le3-sJgozufihuvggvRgA7Hk0RITBa0_WueVLqs-pYLX3fD7t8XCbtaTbm2GwggMP-X5jaOD-bLKxSf3o4FJelJZZpTmHgAevC3UdW7zqiDwhOh0drqbALV9Gn3D65-oZqkBzCzzDVlNUvO0tbj4feXPoERzx6lHpJINr4VAQgID7nA%3D%3D)** We're all looking for the best and most reliable information right now. Signing up for the Expert Insights email is a great way to stay up to speed on the latest data and analysis -- and to help keep your families and communities safe. | [Sign up for Expert Insights](http://i.mikebloomberg.com/u/click?_t=8613ca2a3316453b8f979e9fa5797d01&_m=9ff488ad76364ab8a62bc03486334eb0&_e=-D9OJl4tqk8g2j8DQUwQQj-b2VwKQnkS3BzNo9LQkZv5BwelBSQlk0PZ93oDnwHfvO77DNkOoEwtTZVMCulRk6ooVEStCL3-zzknSG-ugGTaxbJOvIJbYOuAR0KJnEKojjuOMD8Ffi6pTcXzwdqqFca9nYUEZNOpyhGzMkrGqiDrJed5x8XCbc4uTcHHnEjepKeIjAGvbkhoQc6FwT4z7ksnCJcydr0RV6sqSRh5y70V-0pBcONMRduEhtlNgn-Fak0rN4WZ6b5yd-XYv1o7_JB_5de57QzyGh1yU59pmTK4k1gokvuJVq4RZLsGHRZsvlyLow2LHFRIveBepg2fSEcnW0wp5LUBP4VnHr0dJsuQ8dFsqCwecJ0Ot6bQwRWSeDdMh1yZxsGTuF1DJPMRHg%3D%3D)

All the best,

Mike

name: Gina Raimondo
subject: We need talk about crowding at the beach.
body:
Your RI COVID-19 News Update
---
| | | | | |
---
| Hello friend,
I've heard a lot of people talking about the way in which Rhode Island "handled" this virus. When people talk about this in the past tense, that makes me very nervous. Because the truth is, even though it feels like forever, our first case in RI was just over four months ago. We're going to be living with this virus for a year or more, and we're not even halfway there. If we want to be able to keep people safe, to keep businesses open, to send our kids back to school next month, then we need to stay serious.
In that spirit, I want to talk to you about our state beaches.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wYT-tIlmRu6BNAe7Vv--igVyP3PMv4PcDYmtnaMdTF8SlvBMENVdKLaMzmJH-Za0PXCPGxaOpPUZfe_tzMECbTzMVZChWxhctBE5oMgAWeDDHHoKhZ7SmcbuxnKG_WgQDiZUBJgcCDrIatgClKHiTF9xkA1LLm372eD8CKnBvEXJSGdgg3KV_gaRDq5Lcj7ZK1DpCEKY-W4=&c=0BtcEVyqDPKYKsSQkjf3Z2zva9nbmCiV-_ASsbh-TRDnZmCWp4yDvA==&ch=YUM0rIlKMOKRVsSaqHbNzybv4LPwRtGahKaMAvTtDBeQDFR3sPJmsA==)
---
| Rhode Island has some of the best beaches in the country, and it's one of my favorite parts about our state. I grew up going to the beach every weekend in the summer, and now I take my family to the beach. Unfortunately, over the last few weeks we've seen a big increase in the number of people on some of our state beaches. Last month, even with reduced parking, our state beaches saw almost 50,000 more cars than the same time last year. As long as everyone is wearing masks in common areas and social distancing, that's not a problem, but we're seeing a couple places where that's not the case. In particular, we've seen closely packed crowds and poor mask compliance over the past couple weeks at Misquamicut and Scarborough State Beaches. This puts people at risk. Last week, I told Rhode Islanders that, if we don't get beach crowding under control, we're going to have to shut them down again. Unfortunately, last weekend the situation was even worse at these two beaches. The crowding was the worst we've seen it since this crisis began, and that spilled over into crowding at local restaurants and bars. I've been clear from the beginning that the name of the game is flexibility - that we need to be able to adapt our approach and reimpose measures in a targeted way if it becomes necessary. So today I'm announcing that, beginning tomorrow, parking lots at Misquamicut and Scarborough State Beaches will be reduced to 25% capacity. We are dialing back the amount of parking in order to reduce the volume of visitors. This does not mean you should just park elsewhere and walk on to the beach. We'll be working with municipalities to crack down on illegal parking in areas around our beaches, and we'll be ramping up enforcement at local businesses. We'll also have staff out on the beaches to hand out masks to folks who don't have them.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wYT-tIlmRu6BNAe7Vv--igVyP3PMv4PcDYmtnaMdTF8SlvBMENVdKKAJEkO5GbRgvhAxn0tdvrFOBiRmU-N7O_CA54jS3eHcsNWfOU8OI2Sp0Sqy6hSdt7zZyWDkdQ76H6ACnb5OxxMzpH4MAjL_zd8lGDR6XvH66OFuBw47Dimk0Xk05ieETsnbe7HnpBLhhY0t87ALyqA=&c=0BtcEVyqDPKYKsSQkjf3Z2zva9nbmCiV-_ASsbh-TRDnZmCWp4yDvA==&ch=YUM0rIlKMOKRVsSaqHbNzybv4LPwRtGahKaMAvTtDBeQDFR3sPJmsA==)
---
| Click the graph to see check the Department of Health's [COVID-19 data dashboard](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wYT-tIlmRu6BNAe7Vv--igVyP3PMv4PcDYmtnaMdTF8SlvBMENVdKKAJEkO5GbRgvhAxn0tdvrFOBiRmU-N7O_CA54jS3eHcsNWfOU8OI2Sp0Sqy6hSdt7zZyWDkdQ76H6ACnb5OxxMzpH4MAjL_zd8lGDR6XvH66OFuBw47Dimk0Xk05ieETsnbe7HnpBLhhY0t87ALyqA=&c=0BtcEVyqDPKYKsSQkjf3Z2zva9nbmCiV-_ASsbh-TRDnZmCWp4yDvA==&ch=YUM0rIlKMOKRVsSaqHbNzybv4LPwRtGahKaMAvTtDBeQDFR3sPJmsA==).
---
| This does not mean you should just park elsewhere and walk on to the beach. We'll be working with municipalities to crack down on illegal parking in areas around our beaches, and we'll be ramping up enforcement at local businesses. We'll also have staff out on the beaches to hand out masks to folks who don't have them. I want to be transparent: if we start to see these same issues at other beaches, we'll put restrictions on them too. And if we continue to see issues, we'll close our state beach parking lots entirely. I hope it doesn't come to that, and I'm counting on everyone to knock it off. If you drive to a beach and see that it's full, turn around. Go to one of our great parks. Go for a walk or a bike ride. If you're on the beach, set up as far as possible from other people.
-Gina Raimondo Governor
P.S.: Information is our greatest weapon in the fight against COVID-19, If you or someone you know wants to sign up to receive these emails, you can use [this link](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wYT-tIlmRu6BNAe7Vv--igVyP3PMv4PcDYmtnaMdTF8SlvBMENVdKLaMzmJH-Za0r3-q4VvwX40vnuL3CPBw24DLWwkHB93d0CPpe-sgjCIjCSb6_QEArrgqWKfAda30YfXxB29mnVHqD4HuszqZF6v8WnCADDo9YDB8RmHkO0wv0K69KkRAxpzwLsVd4OfL2d9F8Tsszl1Q8blcK2g3gZ7jCghWhSvkxiL3v7Ifs0ZAWMKVM-e6xCbtP2ULd5gs3GFFrCe8SdSue9puRloa-DXMuZf3aiCnhO0zXnLulyrYpvBcgN0ScEh57_PainNxt20L6PLkYVRnpUz7HeJhuLTVKjBrCPG0AMrM8rkzn5TYWQ1UJ7ifRKz6iNf9JwofWgrnXp4Ymq-jGli6ddfnNSPfAl8q55l2KKA9aLWCKj5sJvbA25CQvzvflDpsVbDj&c=0BtcEVyqDPKYKsSQkjf3Z2zva9nbmCiV-_ASsbh-TRDnZmCWp4yDvA==&ch=YUM0rIlKMOKRVsSaqHbNzybv4LPwRtGahKaMAvTtDBeQDFR3sPJmsA==).
---
| | Governor's Office | 82 Smith Street, Providence, RI 02903
---
| [Unsubscribe dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://visitor.constantcontact.com/do?p=un&m=001_rvij1smacIHQplL2vH8YA%3D&ch=e9e38b2e-7e90-11ea-b887-d4ae5292c4bc&ca=44b3d883-312d-4de2-b19c-65314e22d8a7)
---
[Update Profile](https://visitor.constantcontact.com/do?p=oo&m=001_rvij1smacIHQplL2vH8YA%3D&ch=e9e38b2e-7e90-11ea-b887-d4ae5292c4bc&ca=44b3d883-312d-4de2-b19c-65314e22d8a7) | [About Constant Contact](http://www.constantcontact.com/legal/about-constant-contact)
Sent by [communications@governor.ri.gov](mailto:communications@governor.ri.gov) in collaboration with
| [ ](http://www.constantcontact.com/index.jsp?cc=nge&rmc=VF19_3GE)
---

name: Rob Portman
subject: Peter, your thoughts on immigration?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rob Portman for Senate [Unsubscribe](http://links
body:
We have laws for a reason. It’s one thing to change the laws, but it’s an entirely separate thing to erase laws – to completely flip the script...... Peter, **We have laws for a reason. **
It’s one thing to change the laws, but it’s an entirely separate thing to erase laws – to completely flip the script and decriminalize something that the nation has agreed on for decades.
Well, Peter, that’s what many on the Far-Left want to do.

It’s all about progressives on the Squad building themselves up as they tear our country down until it’s unrecognizable. We’re less than four months away from Election Day, and it’s gonna take all of us working together to get her to Washington so she can dismantle their agenda to tear America down. Thank you, Team Claudia Tenney \---- Forwarded Message Below ---- From: Claudia Tenney Subject: We can’t let them dismantle America To: Friend, Did you hear what the Squad was talking about last week? AOC claimed that a $1 billion cut to the NYPD “wasn’t enough”. Meanwhile, Ilhan Omar declared that she intends to tear down our country by “dismantling” our economic and political systems. This is the liberal 2020 agenda in a nutshell. Tear down our police. Tear down our economy. Tear down our history. And tear down the government our Founding Fathers designed. It’s hard to believe that we’ve come to this point, and with less than FOUR MONTHS left until Election Day and our mid-month fundraising deadline coming up this Saturday, time is ticking for us to save America as we know it – because that’s really what’s on the line! I’ve always believed that our freedoms are just as fragile as they are great – and they’re never more than a generation away from extinction. I’m going to Washington to make sure our freedoms live to see another generation, but I need your help to get there first. Thank you for always fighting with me, Claudia Tenney | | | | Paid for by Claudia for Congress | | PO Box 244 Clinton, NY 13323 | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com | | | By law the maximum amount an individual may contribute is $2,800 for the primary and $2,800 for the general election. body:
Now that our restaurants are reopening, let’s support them in a safe and healthy way. [ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=b8feb1ae1e8d475393e82f4d111fced8&_e=5lAQhKdEOcFwudzD6BnTU4RT4glWiU2hvBS2nLs0JC_pALIUqnw1eCM4wG2ah668fhbRZXg8fzPzjmAPcqvuiWnLlkzquWBxejQ8nUp9Dhx9AW79P8fWGStggrAtsW-1CqJlaD1_hZBGaFkDNtbISblIJyM2ol1L5rnh-o-dym3-zCJLfV_5YZDniFJhdo00iorx5EhWFkm4GuDxyexjQMtCc7T3UlqR31_7HzcLYZdrBx3u_NH_ymfDCx3MnNJOM5tLK5nYTplTB8yiS6Gsrvryk3plyOhgq81dpwVxaf5BT6sl2lfgy0ib3QF1OFbOMqp-aL7qrBNTPt-EshuEVi6s4vcWH0dHg6dlhrBuiVQ6J--_WnsPvMdhGubRmZfxmg04px8OntqrW0C2ofuIBEiQtHOPZ-RA0s8smvm6T2KrViXQgVmDMDUGL3wMcc-rZiLYnwzIafgpZKSFr604873e5_VsvyfYaRHNEAsJV30%3D) Friend, Indiana is getting back on track, and businesses are reopening -- including restaurants across the state!
**The COVID-19 pandemic has hit our restaurant industry especially hard. Now that our restaurants are reopening, we’re all excited to get out and support them in a safe and healthy way.** For this week’s One Indiana email, we thought we’d continue sharing some of Eric's favorite places to eat across the state. Last week we pulled together a list his favorite ice cream shops, and now here's a few of his favorite fried chicken places _(no matter your preferred piece, these are all Indiana classics)_.
Remember - as you start to patronize your favorite restaurants and local businesses, be sure to check the **[Indiana State Department of Health website](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=b8feb1ae1e8d475393e82f4d111fced8&_e=5lAQhKdEOcFwudzD6BnTU557RD2d8mP-VdDE4Z6OJ1Ur2bZAHtYxBh4GLkO-9jCWwn2Q0pZFOvSsGXeTWhS-aEQ-OGjZ4V9HryKSagG1f9JI8zwEUgBIenvpx6ZidHL0ESvmGMczYkEZ_Pt3tLCYkqfmj2fQ3xgHO205RD-3NbE2zD8XxhaKyjmz-iDVEgNnBKQ6xHcL--C6ntHnfrrgQ-kQdJvJTEkL1ZGBHKDu8B8vUc9oHxelkq0_tcuyYYeHqmHpRYAJJ5eJJvJCshN-yY9lfv_0lb5m8Zks9rVeqrv-hNmZhIHnZh3vZ5UKF84CELh95_QQ_HqJEyJfQyrpWvMteFQC5WXyaXAWfGebbzDJEuQMYHoBZvoW9tq6GqpJ0HVXPotqp2yTETqpps7d9wU_KM0g_aqmGkGeZ1n26L_vT0DjBs1PYEtT94FT9ua97FJLcwgcs5N3F1XAPmjiJw%3D%3D)**. Local leaders and the businesses in your community might have additional requirements, too, so be sure to check with them, and please follow all guidelines. Check it out -- but another warning like last week, you may get hungry! [ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=b8feb1ae1e8d475393e82f4d111fced8&_e=5lAQhKdEOcFwudzD6BnTU4RT4glWiU2hvBS2nLs0JC_pALIUqnw1eCM4wG2ah668fhbRZXg8fzPzjmAPcqvuiWnLlkzquWBxejQ8nUp9Dhx9AW79P8fWGStggrAtsW-1CqJlaD1_hZBGaFkDNtbISblIJyM2ol1L5rnh-o-dym3-zCJLfV_5YZDniFJhdo00iorx5EhWFkm4GuDxyexjQMtCc7T3UlqR31_7HzcLYZdrBx3u_NH_ymfDCx3MnNJOM5tLK5nYTplTB8yiS6GsrjHqhk5zjjnKcd-OElDd1grsXqT2jdqOCTfLUk--3KllDnXl1q20N2uwQd9-PBrwLjbZ6EUxbt5th1pubAAIdle5gejIKRw-BrU0AWtMYmRJh7B7I1HZZymOnnnY2VQLlqaXM3F2vBEBHWebPgrRVLrqFQd1TJ52Kj1Cc2AJCojPCU-IQj3bro_LB2D73MI9n5TrjLIRIUxQ3klPLljfWtA%3D)
* [**Brau Haus, Oldenburg**](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=b8feb1ae1e8d475393e82f4d111fced8&_e=i_I-ypM6kFNsPWz2PeIhw7RB2EpTszdI0-0WXbTmPLEfziryf3ThSgYC59qWSOt2WDDe6-EbRwQRMWM0RNXpOJtDVD6q3UNHTXVwEQM8uJRi3BbOIZdMjjHJanLXxOGOWQarr2PZYQr9TFrDN57Nf_kyTvLw4UYTn8wjF6UhC8TMyx0z9ex03KESiIGj-z35jiykkuJ5gakdjoS83QfOUj9P86JWbiVI3WAlgOWrprrh9dFPRZvyDXCyT0cFv0kNqLE-ml4t9f5hT-bN9HzSglk-u6ixGjHDbWGH2KqqFh6cAbbWNN5xMSwSD4sYn5Vkmr1DcEbzI1LoL5Cwx3onybCJycAHOaS7NNlScfZzXdd4V-grFBtDqi4uwhGYRwIWFqlLmUrE4wNpZN5CTCOXStaacEGrqunLCQFlNzmk0-U0fSG8edng62P3dHL1yXTp5PROdvQ76XC22KDWZFzi9Q%3D%3D) \- The Brau Haus is home to delicious chicken and a unique past. Check out their unique history and learn a thing or two about Oldenburg.
* **[MCL Restaurant and Bakery, Multiple Locations Statewide](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=b8feb1ae1e8d475393e82f4d111fced8&_e=5lAQhKdEOcFwudzD6BnTU9GAGewXLX9yvyUMVlIzQPnjILpsSWZo7sw4RsXUFkAd4shbxoWukFHgoTyHvyKbuYLqeM6bTrmUOSk0YlNN1SXWWmrhR2R27CQt8R6N_z1EXX8Sd6C4gmMDgeOE5GUDeRJk23akOLv630HmubMeM9ERlsb81AAqZAV1nxDez5Bf4ODPTsLVk8QFpUNGy3D8cYrfWxkVkqSMuxDy13dz5GzkVdyF4w3diZuhcywLTmwepjqy8NSEDQ-mknQoYDCW90Uo-A9we2W3RNUu-eqq5obfPsJP6xQcbrLbjbjCncaSjUiK_de1prKRGZ0RyQO9jMedI01LNBya849hKURaRKE8EMrQ97Vj-JRA61uY-LtMLpsuyOfSaG9lUzQL_G6w_A0RYKf3sDAA69TQ1XtlMkM4jBREJ62mKIaCAfwzZcAHa50cRc_Q6050WKJL-aDudQ%3D%3D)** \- Owned and operated since 1950 by The McGaughey Family, this is the home of the original American comfort food!
* **[Putnam Inn, Greencastle](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=b8feb1ae1e8d475393e82f4d111fced8&_e=5lAQhKdEOcFwudzD6BnTU9k0vSqyzr7QyI_yyPGfNdO1QcDJlTOKuo6vz0axFExdHw6bJy2CTVr72qLUX_F1UgdoZsDQAoHMRtdWczf1n-WzzvDgeX_YQ4Ar691y01LB0QvbXjZyP0aK906zMSffWvLE9w_Rd156aUDRJrpAh0GyPrgGw5GmQM9pBW22nb6cqVxeQcOfDBMNuPEK9-mjZjmO_uO1fRCCSY248Qc6oAVURP9BlsNs6Yj_UhIOcozxuWvjifhEqClkKxltEWzARhULl_3z57a1M9zcgldC4QolgP5inVF7lvOrj6Am--n475sSXX8IP3metuCL1v8RkafXLc04px64Nv5CLrPE-3vWpjQW9Kqc7DVwpi-sU314t5G73WUi5vBLAF6rRJjUMuvKBsjdwMzfHu5zRecrsVgQhKsF2uTRZeoV4kyWn59b)** \- Their slogan says it all — stop by and have a “meal you will never forget!”

Don’t forget to try something from their famous bakery.
* **[The Beef House, Covington](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=b8feb1ae1e8d475393e82f4d111fced8&_e=5lAQhKdEOcFwudzD6BnTU-Gvug-_e2-LbSX_aRIo4U9s6hDfaCpcaUAyYQ6-E2hn8zT_CZly81GTZCN29pJjzINkmvGKzX8xu1ISy_samjLM9jBGgeGxOHg6JIXa5nEWEe498OSt-cv9sB0vG9Nn36JoWSoufC0YnXbmohJoqWNNLuogm8FuG65lzvfWdkYrhOhk6nhi2OsfJAPP_OtPyH4vwW5-JDtCMq7dpbS9ws3wY7veRlyPQWMCjnrja6i5P7QU7ASrLyVJ_GFpQVgnXuYBS99u50wksl4BLFKqcD8Iua5MsXuLzzjOiRP5Zs6wR10HzrXyfNWazLDtD20Jf2yCjWQfN1oH_Vg6bAWCIFYn2VexAtUvj3t-eLEgqTiUgQcklwydRVwfxb2I2TsdNeyQ0xjlfxmM6ckl863XcNJ8eQme5ii9rAfKztsMmCiCsj0H4oHGgNN4bTH4arZiuQ%3D%3D)** \- Family owned & operated since 1964, four generations of the Wright family continue to make the Beef House a special place today

* **[Das Dutchman Essenhaus, Middlebury](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=b8feb1ae1e8d475393e82f4d111fced8&_e=5lAQhKdEOcFwudzD6BnTU2fBdRPzuVHn-EeO24-Ma8OpyieaBO7WiE2FIWmLswnqUG8a6r1oZHDaGN_ekhhTzLsALvRzKF_MaUBlagn3L9TStXu2IS2aGsgCjvPqivzK7gMUq8Lk67thGjQ0UQQIB0IhoQh2ErsS9GyKdROb8p9jQDhLraQCzIrAE0GMW1ODLOa-qNiGqsKOv-G6c5UAgpGKykpJ_AKleq8G58zvAkWFdcw9DVcB1kl35aYWhOtbrpOv66pQBzM1it77YehNYBPwHzVN85zSqhap1zjIylS-aDrGvwHU8XIpt5yKCmTGN-K_qEdxOl2pkSs-kWQeS46Zw-7lG__3djSjQboKBm5RhVJzFzZaIKaHAOzpBPbKA63FqLODcO5lAfzCptTGoLi3bhzZ1jEuOjcuHmtSH21ufbWndopv0nrkGccQt356)** \- The Essenhaus is a destination in Northern Indiana, and you definitely won't leave hungry. * **[Hollyhock Hill, Indianapolis](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=b8feb1ae1e8d475393e82f4d111fced8&_e=i_I-ypM6kFNsPWz2PeIhwzqeSq16rx7Vr6fRgGTWRFKib-viXh7Qp2WiYDyhXjDYY0gZUHgdLz2MeoT85eUK15brcVg74bN2JkHJe4m-88qHbHZOyrMLC1tPAGD8L5FKC2nuyXXH7HWUMhePtSwhnkisuQgF-ilJ17tjtQJPa0EdR3V_gZFU07cYOKpIv9z346iR1o0SMBnvDD2wTnoqQu98tJOTiXetQOPYhJm6GVrhQj3p6owXjr-8BNyaSpF1OfoHPg4qpMNUI9jqBv3hkLlztOj3uPk9Fm6FBYIOGs3trURU-O6cdzGh5R8ciwim6BvSwy8yRnL5llBOMmk5a2YaEL18qA3G9j5L0xaLGHgGHiIZQbzTSSKAuUwOm6FfCYEZx97UZfoctcU3PgY6THCGCR_6qdiRcMlU6syCK4RgJRBNuO7osa2arr-PUzLMislGD4_QOU9ielvXSDPI-w%3D%3D)** \- There's a reason why they've been ranked among the best fried chicken in America. They've been serving their homestyle favorites since 1928!

Thanks for reading our list! No matter whether you’re dining in or ordering take-out, let’s support our Hoosier small businesses.

clean_body: Now that our restaurants are reopening, let’s support them in a safe and healthy way. Friend, Indiana is getting back on track, and businesses are reopening -- including restaurants across the state! The COVID-19 pandemic has hit our restaurant industry especially hard. Now that our restaurants are reopening, we’re all excited to get out and support them in a safe and healthy way. For this week’s One Indiana email, we thought we’d continue sharing some of Eric's favorite places to eat across the state. Last week we pulled together a list his favorite ice cream shops, and now here's a few of his favorite fried chicken places (no matter your preferred piece, these are all Indiana classics). Remember - as you start to patronize your favorite restaurants and local businesses, be sure to check the . Local leaders and the businesses in your community might have additional requirements, too, so be sure to check with them, and please follow all guidelines. Check it out -- but another warning like last week, you may get hungry! \- The Brau Haus is home to delicious chicken and a unique past. Check out their unique history and learn a thing or two about Oldenburg. \- Owned and operated since 1950 by The McGaughey Family, this is the home of the original American comfort food! \- Their slogan says it all — stop by and have a “meal you will never forget!” \- They call the Gasthof Amish Village an "oasis in the farmland" of Southern Indiana. Don’t forget to try something from their famous bakery. \- Family owned & operated since 1964, four generations of the Wright family continue to make the Beef House a special place today \- One of our crew’s favorite haunts, Kramer’s also makes amazing breakfast food! Stop by when you’re in Columbus. \- They’ve been feeding Hoosiers in a beautiful location since 1976! \- A little gem of a restaurant at Brown County State Park, serving fresh home-style meals and Indiana favorites! \- The Leinenbach family has been serving up their great-grandmother’s chicken recipe in Ireland since 1948 — make sure to check it out! \- The Region favorite is now led by its fourth generation. Stop in for many of the same favorites you'd have found there when they first opened their doors more than 90 years ago (like their homemade fried chicken). \- This family-owned restaurant is a piece of Indy history. It sadly suffered a fire in January, but the owners have plans to rebuild and bring this favorite back to life! \- The Blue Gate's got it all — great Amish and Mennonite cooking, entertainment, shopping, a hotel and more. \- The Essenhaus is a destination in Northern Indiana, and you definitely won't leave hungry. After you've had your chicken, stop by the bakery. \- There's a reason why they've been ranked among the best fried chicken in America. They've been serving their homestyle favorites since 1928! Thanks for reading our list! No matter whether you’re dining in or ordering take-out, let’s support our Hoosier small businesses. Did we miss your favorite fried chicken place in Indiana? Let us know using the comment box below! Thanks for reading — we’ll be in touch soon. -Your Holcomb Crew | | | | | | Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana. © Copyright 2020. All rights reserved. | | Eric Holcomb for Indiana, 101 W Ohio Street, Suite 2200, Indianapolis, IN 46204 | (317) 569-0709 | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com | | |

subject: Trump Thinks Texas Isn't In Play, but OUR 3 CANDIDATES WON BIGLY!
body:
We Had Many Successful Wins In Texas Last Night, proving that when we expand the electorate we can win Texas with Progressives on the ballot! | | | | [ ](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=91ce69546d&e=66979298f9)
---
| | |
# **TEXAS IS TURNING BLUE**
---
|
### **Trump doesn't think Texas is in play, but Future Generations is expanding the electorate!**
---
| **[Donate now to expand the electorate in Texas! If you've already given, give again and share with your friends!](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=7d33abe9ea&e=66979298f9)**

| [Donate Now to Flip Texas!](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=306e35a8c1&e=66979298f9 "Donate Now to Flip Texas!")

---
|
---
|
# MEET OUR TEXAS WINNERS!
---
| _All our candidates won their Democratic runoff elections in Texas and are heading to the November General!_

[**_We need you to win in November!_**](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=f820fb4118&e=66979298f9)
---
| [ ](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=39c277c11f&e=66979298f9)
---
#### **Mike Seigel** is running for U.S. Congress in Texas's 10th District. Mike is back and ready to finish what he started in 2018 by giving, 15 year incumbent, Republican Michael McCaul a run for his dirty money. Mike's a civil rights attorney and former public school teacher, and is fighting for the People in northeast Texas. Mike stands strong and will fight for labor rights, education reform, immigration reform, Medicare For All, and a Green New Deal for future generations!
| [ ](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=8851384029&e=66979298f9)
---
#### **Lorenzo Sanchez** is running for House District 67. he's an unyielding champion for marginalized communities. Lorenzo’s values are centered from the early struggles of his mother, who left Mexico as a child in search of her family. Seeing his mother working tirelessly and at times going hungry to make sure her children ate, inspired Lorenzo to fight for our most vulnerable. We are proud to endorse Lorenzo along with Sunrise Movement Dallas, because he will champion for a Future Generations with a bold GND plan and an inclusive economy that leaves not one Texan behind.
| [ ](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=ecc7f54aae&e=66979298f9)
---
#### **Jasmine Crockett** is running for House District 100. Jasmine Crockett is a Green New Deal champion fighting for clean air and water, housing justice, and environmental protections for all communities. Jasmine understands that environmental racism can only be dismantled with a Green New Deal that centers all frontline communities. Jasmine is also a criminal defense and civil rights attorney who has represented families in Texas who've been victims to a failing system.
|
---
| | Due to COVID-19 health and safety restrictions, we must focus on phone and text banking to get our candidates message to their constituents.**[ Will you invest today for seven generations into the future?](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=8db60cdef2&e=66979298f9)**
---
| [](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=8f2539d896&e=66979298f9)
---
| **_WE USE EVERY PENNY EFFECTIVELY _**
Together we texted over 40,000 voters and ensured that both Jasmine and Lorenzo made it through very close primary races, winning by less than 200 votes each! Our powerful Texas volunteer team likely made the difference in ensuring their success when the results came in last night. Because we are volunteer powered, **it cost us just a few hundred dollars to send all 40,000 texts to voters**, and you chip in to help us cover the costs so we can keep growing!
---
| [DONATE TO HELP COVER TEXTING COSTS](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=442b51da49&e=66979298f9 "DONATE TO HELP COVER TEXTING COSTS")
---
|

clean_body: We Had Many Successful Wins In Texas Last Night, proving that when we expand the electorate we can win Texas with Progressives on the ballot! | | | | | | | TEXAS IS TURNING BLUE | Trump doesn't think Texas is in play, but Future Generations is expanding the electorate! | | "Donate Now to Flip Texas!" | | | "Give $7 Now to Flip Texas!" | | MEET OUR TEXAS WINNERS! | All our candidates won their Democratic runoff elections in Texas and are heading to the November General! | Mike Seigel is running for U.S. Congress in Texas's 10th District. Mike is back and ready to finish what he started in 2018 by giving, 15 year incumbent, Republican Michael McCaul a run for his dirty money. Mike's a civil rights attorney and former public school teacher, and is fighting for the People in northeast Texas. Mike stands strong and will fight for labor rights, education reform, immigration reform, Medicare For All, and a Green New Deal for future generations! | Lorenzo Sanchez is running for House District 67. he's an unyielding champion for marginalized communities. Lorenzo’s values are centered from the early struggles of his mother, who left Mexico as a child in search of her family. Seeing his mother working tirelessly and at times going hungry to make sure her children ate, inspired Lorenzo to fight for our most vulnerable. We are proud to endorse Lorenzo along with Sunrise Movement Dallas, because he will champion for a Future Generations with a bold GND plan and an inclusive economy that leaves not one Texan behind. | Jasmine Crockett is running for House District 100. Jasmine Crockett is a Green New Deal champion fighting for clean air and water, housing justice, and environmental protections for all communities. Jasmine understands that environmental racism can only be dismantled with a Green New Deal that centers all frontline communities. Jasmine is also a criminal defense and civil rights attorney who has represented families in Texas who've been victims to a failing system. | | | Due to COVID-19 health and safety restrictions, we must focus on phone and text banking to get our candidates message to their constituents. | | WE USE EVERY PENNY EFFECTIVELY Together we texted over 40,000 voters and ensured that both Jasmine and Lorenzo made it through very close primary races, winning by less than 200 votes each! Our powerful Texas volunteer team likely made the difference in ensuring their success when the results came in last night. Because we are volunteer powered, it cost us just a few hundred dollars to send all 40,000 texts to voters, and you chip in to help us cover the costs so we can keep growing! | "DONATE TO HELP COVER TEXTING COSTS" | | MEET OUR FUTURE GEN TEAM: CHIARA D'ANGELO | | As the National Campaigns Director I am wholeheartedly committed to serving as an ally to my Indigenous leadership and ensuring that all of our candidates get the support they deserve. I am honored to serve in this role and hope you will join me in showing up for the inspiring candidates our PAC is supporting. name: MJ Hegar HQ
subject: The long road ahead
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
Now that the runoff is over, we've got to rapidly build this team.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890108/7f51f7bc/41fbb10a/4ef66863/2862743974/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | | **SUPPORTER RECORD**
Supporter: Peter
Donated: Not yet!*
Goal: **750 new donors**
Deadline: Midnight

**[DONATE](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890108/7f51f7bc/41fbb10a/4ef66862/2862743974/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3MjMifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-postrunoffb?refcode=email_20200715.3nd__1723__&amount=10")**
---
Hey Peter -- over 56,000 donors have already pitched in to MJ's campaign so far, and it's thanks to them that we had the resources to win the runoff!

Now that the runoff is over, we're facing an even _**bigger**_ challenge -- taking on Sen. Cornyn and his big money donors in the general election! This means we've got to ramp up our goals and build out our grassroots team with new* donors like you. We've broken down our 750 new donor goal, and we need 15 new donors from your community to stay on pace. **[Can we count on you?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890108/7f51f7bc/41fbb10a/4ef66862/2862743974/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3MjMifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-postrunoffb?refcode=email_20200715.3nd__1723__&amount=10")**

Every new donor is another step toward beating Sen. Cornyn and ensuring that we elect MJ to fight for our health care, our environment, our jobs, and our children.

**Will you pitch in toward our goal and be one of the new donors from your community we need to help us reach our goal?**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
---
**[Express donate $5>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890108/7f51f7bc/41fbb10a/4ef66861/2862743974/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3MjMifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-postrunoffb?express_lane=true&refcode=email_20200715.3nd__1723__&amount=5")**
**[Express donate $10>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890108/7f51f7bc/41fbb10a/4ef66860/2862743974/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3MjMifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-postrunoffb?express_lane=true&refcode=email_20200715.3nd__1723__&amount=10")**
**[Express donate $25>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890108/7f51f7bc/41fbb10a/4ef66867/2862743974/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3MjMifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-postrunoffb?express_lane=true&refcode=email_20200715.3nd__1723__&amount=25")**
**[Express donate $50>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890108/7f51f7bc/41fbb10a/4ef66866/2862743974/VEsB/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3MjMifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-postrunoffb?express_lane=true&refcode=email_20200715.3nd__1723__&amount=50")**
**[Or, donate a different amount>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890108/7f51f7bc/41fbb10a/4ef66865/2862743974/VEsO/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3MjMifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-postrunoffb?refcode=email_20200715.3nd__1723__")**
\-- Team MJ

_*If you've already contributed to the campaign, thank you so much! Our records are based on donations associated with this email address in our online database, which may take some time to update._
**[DONATE](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890108/7f51f7bc/41fbb10a/4ef66862/2862743974/VEsP/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3MjMifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-postrunoffb?refcode=email_20200715.3nd__1723__&amount=10")**

|
---

| It's clear we need a real leader for Texas and a Senate that prioritizes our health care. **[More than ever, we're relying on online fundraising to keep building the campaign we need to win in November, and we greatly appreciate your continued support>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890108/7f51f7bc/41fbb10a/4ef66864/2862743974/VEsHBQ/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3MjMifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-covidfooter?refcode=1723&amount=10")**

However, we know this is not an easy time for many. If you'd like to pause receiving fundraising emails for the next few weeks, **[please let us know here](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890108/7f51f7bc/41fbb10a/4ef6685b/2862743974/VEsHBA/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/s/mj-covid-fewer-emails")**. We'll still keep in touch with important information and to ask your thoughts from time to time.
---

Round Rock, TX 78683

name: ChristyforCongress.org
subject: ECU vs. Citizens United
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress
body:
| | [](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6babb/29776869/9e652dd/569efbc1/3646906987/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTVfTU0yY2l0dW5pdGVkLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2007_email_christy_csgoal?refcode=em20_200715_MM2citunited-full")
Peter,

If you want to know who's going to represent people and who's going to represent corporations -- look no further than these endorsements.

Mike Garcia has been endorsed by Citizens United -- the conservative PAC that sued the FEC so they could accept unlimited cash from corporations. They're the reason we have super PACs.

Christy, on the other hand, is endorsed by End Citizens United, which is dedicated to overturning that disastrous ruling and cleaning up our elections. **That's why she doesn't take corporate PAC money.**

These are the types of contrasts we'll be highlighting in Christy's candidate statement (a blurb that goes in official voter guides from the state). But the cost to file isn't cheap -- we need to raise $17,000 by midnight on Thursday to pay for it.

**[Will you pitch in $5 or more to help hit our $17,000 goal and make sure CA-25 voters know Christy can't be bought by special interests?](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6babb/29776869/9e652dd/569efbc1/3646906987/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTVfTU0yY2l0dW5pdGVkLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2007_email_christy_csgoal?refcode=em20_200715_MM2citunited-full")**

[](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6babb/29776869/9e652dd/569efbc1/3646906987/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTVfTU0yY2l0dW5pdGVkLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2007_email_christy_csgoal?refcode=em20_200715_MM2citunited-full") The people of California's 25th district a representative fights for them -- not corporations, billionaires, or special interests. That's Christy.

Mike Garcia's plan to win is to rake in hundreds of thousands from corporate PACs and hope super PACs and dark money groups flood the airwaves for him. And that's who he'll represent.

Our plan is different -- no money from corporate PACs or D.C. lobbyists. Instead, raise from the people Christy will represent, regular folks pitching in whatever they can afford -- which adds up to big change.

**But that also means every grassroots donation is critical.**

**[Peter, can you pitch in $5 to help hit our $17,000 Candidate Statement goal? We'll only get there with your help.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6babb/29776869/9e652dd/569efbc1/3646906987/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTVfTU0yY2l0dW5pdGVkLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2007_email_christy_csgoal?refcode=em20_200715_MM2citunited-full")** [YES](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6babb/29776869/9e652dd/569efbc1/3646906987/VEsD/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTVfTU0yY2l0dW5pdGVkLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2007_email_christy_csgoal?refcode=em20_200715_MM2citunited-full") Let's go win this thing,

\-- ChristyforCongress.org

|
[](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6babb/29776869/9e652dd/569efbc1/3646906987/VEsA/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTVfTU0yY2l0dW5pdGVkLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2007_email_christy_csgoal?refcode=em20_200715_MM2citunited-full")
Christy Smith is a community leader from CA-25 who has fought for her neighbors in Ventura and LA county on the school board and in the California state legislature. Now she needs our help to defeat Mike Garcia and flip this top pick-up House seat blue in November. Help elect a champion for our health care, our schools, and our rights. [DONATE](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6babb/29776869/9e652dd/569efbc1/3646906987/VEsB/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTVfTU0yY2l0dW5pdGVkLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2007_email_christy_csgoal?refcode=em20_200715_MM2citunited-full")
---

|
This email was sent by **[Christy Smith](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6babb/29776869/9e652dd/569efbc2/3646906987/VEsO/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://www.christyforcongress.org/")**
PO Box 301, 24307 Magic Mountain Pkwy, Valencia, CA 91355.

If you no longer want to hear from Christy Smith, [**click here to get off all our lists instantly.**](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6babb/29776869/9e652dd/569efbc3/3646906987/VEsP/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.christyforcongress.org/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com") Questions or concerns? name: Raphael Warnock
subject: Representation matters.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warnock for Georgia
body:
If we flip Georgia and Mississippi blue, we can finally sweep the South and make sure everyone has a voice in our democracy.
| | |
---
Friend: Right now, we have a historic opportunity to change the face of leadership in our country - especially __ in the South. **That's why I'm reaching out to you about my friend, Mike Espy, who's running for Senate in Mississippi. **
Mike has spent his entire career fighting for working families as a congressman and as U.S. Secretary of Agriculture, and if elected, he would be Mississippi's first Black senator since Reconstruction.
In 2018, Mike got closer than anybody in nearly 40 years to flipping Mississippi blue - and now, he and I are building on the foundation he set to finally sweep the South and create a new, bluer one. **But our Republican opponents are spending _millions_ to defeat us, and we won't be able to win in Mississippi and Georgia without your critical grassroots support:**

**[Will you split a donation right now between me and Mike Espy so we can win in Georgia and Mississippi and take back the Senate for Democrats?](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.com/page/m/60b3e5c0/288179be/849ec80/348e6d4d/3679882875/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoid2Fybm9jay1mb3ItZ2VvcmdpYV9lbWFpbC1lc3B5IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJqdGs0MjctZnItbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiNSwyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCIsImUzc2dZV0p5WldOMWNuSnBibWNnZlgwPSI6IiJ9/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/warnock-for-georgia_email-espy?refcode=jtk427-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
[](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.com/page/m/60b3e5c0/288179be/849ec80/348e6d4d/3679882875/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoid2Fybm9jay1mb3ItZ2VvcmdpYV9lbWFpbC1lc3B5IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJqdGs0MjctZnItbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiNSwyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCIsImUzc2dZV0p5WldOMWNuSnBibWNnZlgwPSI6IiJ9/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/warnock-for-georgia_email-espy?refcode=jtk427-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")
Representation _matters_ , friend. While Georgia and Mississippi have some of the largest Black populations in the country, the Deep South has only ever sent _three _Black leaders to the Senate. Mike and I are running to right that wrong - and to hold the very same offices that have historically prevented people like us from having a seat at the table.
The South deserves leaders who fight to give everyone a voice in our democracy - not politicians like Sen. Hyde-Smith (R-MS), who has made jokes about public hangings and voter suppression, or like Sen. Loeffler (R-GA), who's leading an all-out assault on the Black Lives Matter movement.
**That's why Mike and I are fighting to make history as Mississippi's first Black senator since Reconstruction and as**** Georgia's first Black senator _ever_. But our Republican opponents are spending millions in special interest money to defeat us and keep the Senate in GOP control - and there's no way we can flip these seats and take back the Senate for Democrats without your immediate support.**
**[That's why I'm personally asking: Will you split an urgent donation between my campaign and Mike Espy's before midnight tonight to fuel our historic campaigns, flip the Senate and power us through the most important election of our lifetimes?](https://secure.warnockforgeorgia.com/page/m/60b3e5c0/288179be/849ec80/348e6d4d/3679882875/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoid2Fybm9jay1mb3ItZ2VvcmdpYV9lbWFpbC1lc3B5IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJqdGs0MjctZnItbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiNSwyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCIsImUzc2dZV0p5WldOMWNuSnBibWNnZlgwPSI6IiJ9/?g=3wiJLcQot1BeM8zu13FVD5A "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/warnock-for-georgia_email-espy?refcode=jtk427-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
Thank you,

Reverend Warnock

clean_body: If we flip Georgia and Mississippi blue, we can finally sweep the South and make sure everyone has a voice in our democracy. | | | Friend: Right now, we have a historic opportunity to change the face of leadership in our country - especially in the South. That's why I'm reaching out to you about my friend, Mike Espy, who's running for Senate in Mississippi. Mike has spent his entire career fighting for working families as a congressman and as U.S. Secretary of Agriculture, and if elected, he would be Mississippi's first Black senator since Reconstruction. In 2018, Mike got closer than anybody in nearly 40 years to flipping Mississippi blue - and now, he and I are building on the foundation he set to finally sweep the South and create a new, bluer one. But our Republican opponents are spending millions to defeat us, and we won't be able to win in Mississippi and Georgia without your critical grassroots support: "" "" Representation matters , friend. While Georgia and Mississippi have some of the largest Black populations in the country, the Deep South has only ever sent three Black leaders to the Senate. Mike and I are running to right that wrong - and to hold the very same offices that have historically prevented people like us from having a seat at the table. The South deserves leaders who fight to give everyone a voice in our democracy - not politicians like Sen. Hyde-Smith (R-MS), who has made jokes about public hangings and voter suppression, or like Sen. Loeffler (R-GA), who's leading an all-out assault on the Black Lives Matter movement. That's why Mike and I are fighting to make history as Mississippi's first Black senator since Reconstruction and as Georgia's first Black senator ever. But our Republican opponents are spending millions in special interest money to defeat us and keep the Senate in GOP control - and there's no way we can flip these seats and take back the Senate for Democrats without your immediate support. "" Thank you, Reverend Warnock | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. "" | | (C) 2020 | Paid for by Warnock for Georgia. Title and affiliation are provided for identification purposes only. name: Joshua Scott for Congress
subject: BREAKING: Covina Republican office vandalized; brick thrown through window, no injuries.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Joshua Scott for Congress
body:
Image

https://clicks.aweber.com/y/ct/?l=GSTzK&m=gTeINXZz22fBCP.&b=47pugUoP4ce7nGyApHA.nw

For Immediate Release

July 15, 2020

Joshua Scott for Congress

(626) 214-5406

info@votejoshuascott.org

BREAKING NEWS: Covina Republican office vandalized; brick thrown through window, no injuries.

During the early morning hours of July 15, a brick was thrown through the front window of the East San Gabriel Valley Republican Center in Covina, CA. Thankfully, nobody was injured during this criminal act of vandalism. Covina Police Department promptly arrived on scene and is currently investigating security footage.

The Center is a non-profit office that serves as a venue for political education, volunteering, and as an office for the Joshua Scott for Congress campaign. The Center is not affiliated with any political party.

Joshua Scott, the Republican nominee for California's 32nd congressional district said the following regarding the vandalism:

"The only surprise that comes out of this despicable criminal act of vandalism is the fact that it did not happen sooner. Over the past two months, we have seen many left-wing officials in Los Angeles and beyond remain completely silent on the violent destruction of countless local businesses and places of worship, while citing these events as peaceful protests. The unhindered destruction of our cities, compounded by the spewing of anti-police rhetoric from our elected officials and media, has emboldened the radical, anti-American, left-wing mob to continue its atrocities. Though unfortunate and disheartening, our campaign will never stop fighting for American freedom, liberty, and equality for all. We will continue to project unapologetic patriotism, stand up for what is right, support our brave police officers, and flip this beautiful district in the East San Gabriel Valley."

Photographs of the vandalism:

Image

https://clicks.aweber.com/y/ct/?l=GSTzK&m=gTeINXZz22fBCP.&b=47pugUoP4ce7nGyApHA.nw

Image

https://clicks.aweber.com/y/ct/?l=GSTzK&m=gTeINXZz22fBCP.&b=47pugUoP4ce7nGyApHA.nw

Image

https://clicks.aweber.com/y/ct/?l=GSTzK&m=gTeINXZz22fBCP.&b=47pugUoP4ce7nGyApHA.nw

The East San Gabriel Valley Republican Center is located in the heart of District 32, which contains Azusa, Baldwin Park, Covina, Duarte, El Monte, Glendora, Industry, Irwindale, La Puente, La Verne, Monrovia, San Dimas, and West Covina.

For more information about the Joshua Scott for Congress campaign, visit votejoshuascott.org

https://clicks.aweber.com/y/ct/?l=GSTzK&m=gTeINXZz22fBCP.&b=47pugUoP4ce7nGyApHA.nw
.

Donate to Joshua Scott for Congress

Covina CA 91723

name: Jon Ossoff (personal)
subject: Re: Trump's visit today
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JON OSSOFF FOR SENATE
body:
He came for a taxpayer-funded photo op with my opponent. |
---
Friend, I wanted to write to you personally to reflect on Donald Trump's visit to Georgia today.

**Georgia's hospitalizations are skyrocketing, thousands are dead, and millions of Georgians are out of work, facing eviction or foreclosure, and in need of fast economic relief.**

**Yet instead of presenting a plan to solve this unprecedented health and economic crisis, Trump flew down on Air Force One today for a taxpayer-funded photo op with my opponent, Sen. David Perdue.**

_The President barely mentioned the pandemic. Frankly, the whole scene was surreal._

Trump wasn't wearing a mask, the crowd was packed in, and the President spent most of his time raging against mail-in ballots and boasting about infrastructure, an issue he and my opponent have neglected for *three and a half years.*

**Friend, Donald Trump and David Perdue are living in the twilight zone. They're in denial and out of control. They can't deal with reality. They can't bring themselves to address the greatest crisis our country has faced in decades, which has spiraled out of control.**

MEANWHILE ... while Trump was ignoring the pandemic today and praising David Perdue, I held a virtual town hall to answer voters' questions about COVID-19 in Georgia.

**I said it during the town hall and I will say it again to you, now: America should be the world leader in fighting the virus.**

But, tragically our leaders have totally failed us.
**Now, more than ever, it's time for new leadership in Washington.**
**[So will you make a $10 donation to send Senator David Perdue into retirement and secure a majority in the U.S. Senate?](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e2140/5b2c1286/3db5aa8e/25f14968/987507263/VEsH/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_07_15_trump_visited_ga?refcode=em_2020_07_15_trump_visited_ga&amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100,500,1000,2800")**

| | [Chip in $10 to my campaign >>](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e2140/5b2c1286/3db5aa8e/25f14968/987507263/VEsE/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_07_15_trump_visited_ga?refcode=em_2020_07_15_trump_visited_ga&amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100,500,1000,2800")
---

For Donald Trump to visit our state -- where hospitalizations are surging -- just to ignore the public health crisis and score a campaign photo op is shameful.

To fly here and _not even address_ the more than 123,000 confirmed cases and 3,000 deaths in Georgia is absurd.

**Friend, you and I both know Trump really came because he's terrified of a humiliating defeat in Georgia.**

**_Where is their plan to address the coronavirus? Where is their plan to get help to American families whose household finances are on the brink, or worse, because of this unmitigated disaster?_**

They would both rather pretend coronavirus will "just disappear" (Trump's words) than deal with reality. Because reality doesn't fit into their narrative.

REMEMBER ... In January and February, my opponent, Senator Perdue, was trading medical stocks while publicly downplaying the threat of COVID-19 -- too busy lining his own pockets to prepare his constituents for a pandemic.

_When Trump said today that Perdue is "a great Senator" who has "done a phenomenal job," is this what he was talking about?_

Just yesterday, the Trump administration ordered hospitals to bypass the CDC and send key COVID-19 data directly to Washington instead, where its credibility will be in doubt and could be manipulated to serve partisan ends. _That's outrageous._

**I promise you, friend, when I am in the Senate, I will lead and serve informed by science and facts. I will put public safety first and create health policy based on medical expertise, not my financial or political interests.**

**[Right now, if you can, please contribute $10 or any amount to help me defeat Trump's allies in Georgia and put Democrats in control of the U.S. Senate.](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e2140/5b2c1286/3db5aa8e/25f14968/987507263/VEsF/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_07_15_trump_visited_ga?refcode=em_2020_07_15_trump_visited_ga&amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100,500,1000,2800")**

| [Chip in $10 immediately >>](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e2140/5b2c1286/3db5aa8e/25f14968/987507263/VEsC/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_07_15_trump_visited_ga?refcode=em_2020_07_15_trump_visited_ga&amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100,500,1000,2800")
---

---
| [Chip in $50 immediately >>](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e2140/5b2c1286/3db5aa8e/25f1496a/987507263/VEsA/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_07_15_trump_visited_ga?refcode=em_2020_07_15_trump_visited_ga&amount=50&amounts=10,25,50,100,500,1000,2800")
---
| [Chip in $100 immediately >>](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e2140/5b2c1286/3db5aa8e/25f1496d/987507263/VEsB/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_07_15_trump_visited_ga?refcode=em_2020_07_15_trump_visited_ga&amount=100&amounts=10,25,50,100,500,1000,2800")

I'm grateful to have you on the team,

Jon
